title: pinguicula corsica
intro: Pinguicula corsica, also known as the Corsican butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region, where it thrives in moist, humid environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pinguicula corsica include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved breathing.
